parsed.refined_request: Two VMs (vm_us_central1, vm_europe_west2), Linux 2 CPU / 4 GB / 50 GB, connected to l2_shared_network (IPv4). After relaxation: both co-located in us-central1, conflict resolved.

conflicts.explanation: An L2 network (same broadcast domain) cannot span multiple geographic regions (us-central1 and europe-west2) in any cloud paradigm.

relaxations.action: Replace the L2 network with a cloud-native global L3 network (Global VPC + peering/interconnect).
relaxations.explanation: Enables cross-region communication, but no longer a true L2 broadcast domain (L2->L3).

relaxations.action: Deploy both VMs in the same region (relocate vm_europe_west2 to us-central1).
relaxations.explanation: Maintains the L2 broadcast domain; sacrifices geographic separation (regions->same_region).

relaxations.action: Use a third-party L2 VPN / SD-WAN to extend L2 across regions.
relaxations.explanation: Preserves L2 across regions but adds complexity, latency, cost.

parsed.applied_relaxation: Deploy both VMs in the same region (us-central1)  [regions->same_region]
parsed.tosca_template:
tosca_definitions_version: tosca_simple_yaml_1_3
description: Two Linux VMs co-located in us-central1, connected to a shared IPv4 network.
topology_template:
  node_templates:
    vm_us_central1:
      type: computeWithnetwork
    vm_europe_west2:
      type: computeWithnetwork
    l2_shared_network:
      type: Network
      properties:
        ip_version: 4
  policies:
  - placement_group_1:
      type: acme.policies.Placement
      targets: [vm_us_central1, vm_europe_west2]
      properties:
        locations:
        - region: us-central1

note_eval: Version reglee: option co-localisation (regions->same) appliquee -> conflit resolu, VMs en us-central1. Detection OK + 2 relax valides + validite fonctionnelle OK.
